questions.question: Where can members and workspace owners add new connections to their workspace?

options.text: Settings → Integrations
options.explanation: The correct path is Settings → Connections. Integrations is a different concept related to API development.

options.text: Settings → Connections
options.explanation: You navigate to Settings in the left sidebar, then select the Connections tab to add new connections.

options.text: Workspace → Members
options.explanation: There is no 'Members' tab in the main workspace settings where connections are managed.

options.text: API Dashboard → Create New
options.explanation: Connections are managed directly in the Notion UI settings, not via the API dashboard for end-users.

questions.question: How do you add a connection to a specific page in Notion?

options.text: Navigate to the page and click 'Add connections' in the bottom right corner.
options.explanation: The option is located in the '•••' menu at the top right of the page, not the bottom right.

options.text: Navigate to a page, select ••• at the top right, and click 'Add connections'.
options.explanation: This is the correct workflow: open the page menu, select 'Add connections', and search for the connection.

options.text: Go to Settings → Connections and select the page from a list.
options.explanation: Connections must be added to the specific page via the page's menu; you don't select pages from a global settings list.

options.text: Hover over the connection name in the sidebar and click 'Activate'.
options.explanation: Connections must be explicitly added to a page via the menu to be active on that specific page.

questions.question: In the 'Settings → Connections' menu, which tab is used to install and manage Security & Compliance integrations for an entire Enterprise workspace?

options.text: The Members tab
options.explanation: The Members tab is for managing integrations that members can access and limiting their list to approved integrations.

options.text: The Workspaces tab
options.explanation: The Workspaces tab is specifically for installing and managing Security & Compliance integrations across the enterprise.

options.text: The API tab
options.explanation: The API tab is for developers to generate tokens, not for installing security integrations.

options.text: The Integrations tab
options.explanation: There is no 'Integrations' tab in this specific settings context; it is split into Members and Workspaces.

questions.question: When managing page access for an integration on an Enterprise plan, what is the difference between selecting 'Default' and 'Workspace owners'?

options.text: Default restricts access to owners only, while Workspace owners allows all members.
options.explanation: The opposite is true: Default allows all workspace owners and members to manage access, whereas Workspace owners restricts it to owners.

options.text: Default allows all workspace owners and members to manage access, while Workspace owners restricts it to only workspace owners.
options.explanation: Selecting 'Default' gives both owners and members permission to manage page access. Selecting 'Workspace owners' limits this power to owners exclusively.

options.text: They function the same way; there is no difference in permissions.
options.explanation: The two options serve different purposes regarding who has the administrative rights to connect or disconnect integrations on pages.

options.text: Default is for public integrations, and Workspace owners is for private integrations.
options.explanation: This setting refers to user permission levels for management, not the visibility or type of the integration itself.

questions.question: What options are available in the '•••' menu next to a connection in the workspace settings?

options.text: Edit permissions, Delete workspace, View logs, and Restart service
options.explanation: The available options are Retrieve an internal API token, Visit the developer's website or contact their support, View users with access to the connection, and Disconnect the connection.

options.text: Retrieve an internal API token, Visit the developer's website, View users with access, and Disconnect the connection
options.explanation: This menu allows you to manage the connection's lifecycle, retrieve its token, find support, see users, or remove it.

options.text: Copy API key, Share connection, Rename integration, and Enable webhooks
options.explanation: While webhooks are mentioned elsewhere, they are not options in this specific '•••' menu for existing connections.

options.text: Install partner platform, Generate OAuth token, and Grant admin rights
options.explanation: These actions are handled during the installation phase or via partner platforms, not via this specific workspace settings menu.

questions.question: On which plan is the feature to decide what pages an integration can access available?

options.text: Free Plan
options.explanation: This specific page access management feature is only available to users on the Enterprise Plan.

options.text: Pro Plan
options.explanation: The page access management feature is not available on the Pro Plan; it is exclusive to Enterprise.

options.text: Enterprise Plan
options.explanation: Workspace owners on the Enterprise Plan can decide what pages an integration can access and who can manage that access.

options.text: Team Plan
options.explanation: There is no specific 'Team Plan' tier in this context; the feature is restricted to Enterprise.

questions.question: How do you remove a connection from a page once it has been added?

options.text: Go to Settings → Connections and click 'Disconnect' next to the page name.
options.explanation: You must remove the connection directly from the specific page's menu, not from the global workspace settings.

options.text: Hover over the connection's name in the page menu and press 'Disconnect'.
options.explanation: The instruction is to hover over the connection's name in the '•••' menu and press Disconnect to remove it from that page.

options.text: Delete the page to remove the connection.
options.explanation: Deleting the page is a drastic measure; you can simply disconnect the connection from the page without deleting the page itself.

options.text: Click 'Remove' in the top right corner of the page.
options.explanation: There is no 'Remove' button for connections in the top right corner; you must use the 'Disconnect' action within the connection menu.

questions.question: When installing a partner integration via OAuth (e.g., Zapier or Typeform), what is a key characteristic of the connection?

options.text: Connections are global and apply to all workspaces automatically.
options.explanation: Connections created via OAuth are workspace-specific. You must authenticate and select pages for the specific workspace you are in.

options.text: You do not need to select pages; the connection has access to everything by default.
options.explanation: After authenticating, you are presented with a list of pages in the selected workspace and must choose which ones the connection can access.

options.text: Connections are only available on the Free plan.
options.explanation: OAuth connections can be installed on any plan, though some specific management features are restricted to Enterprise.

options.text: The connection must be installed from the Notion API dashboard first.
options.explanation: OAuth connections are installed directly through the partner platform's interface (e.g., Zapier), not via the Notion API dashboard.

questions.question: How do you link an existing connection to a partner platform that requires an internal integration token?

options.text: Generate a new token in the partner platform's settings and paste it into Notion.
options.explanation: You must copy the existing internal integration token from the connection's menu in Notion and paste it into the partner platform's form.

options.text: Navigate to Settings → API and request a token from the developer.
options.explanation: You are not requesting a new token; you are using the one already associated with the existing connection in your workspace.

options.text: Copy the internal integration token from the connection's '•••' menu and paste it into the partner platform's form.
options.explanation: The '•••' menu offers the option to 'Retrieve an internal API token', which you then copy to the partner platform.

options.text: Contact Notion Support to generate the token for you.
options.explanation: You handle the token retrieval yourself; Notion does not generate it for you in this context.

questions.question: Who can add connections to a workspace in 'Settings' → 'Connections'?

options.text: Only Workspace Owners
options.explanation: Both Members and Workspace Owners have the ability to add connections to the workspace.

options.text: Only Members
options.explanation: Members can add connections, but Workspace Owners can also do so.

options.text: Members and Workspace Owners
options.explanation: The article states that both Members and Workspace Owners can add connections to a workspace in this menu.

options.text: Only Administrators
options.explanation: The terminology 'Administrators' is not used; the distinction is between Members and Workspace Owners.

questions.question: Which of the following restrictions applies to Workspace Owners on Enterprise Plans?

options.text: Owners cannot remove connections from pages once they are added.
options.explanation: Owners have full management privileges; they can disconnect integrations from pages as part of their role.

options.text: The ability to add connections and decide who can connect/disconnect at the page level.
options.explanation: On Enterprise Plans, Workspace Owners can restrict the ability to add connections and manage access at the page level.

options.text: Owners cannot view who has access to a specific connection.
options.explanation: Owners can view users with access to a connection via the '•••' menu.

options.text: Owners cannot install Security & Compliance integrations.
options.explanation: Owners can install and manage Security & Compliance integrations across the entire workspace.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'Members' tab in 'Settings → Connections'?

options.text: To view and disconnect integrations used by workspace members.
options.explanation: The Members tab allows you to see which integrations members are using and to disconnect them, as well as limit access to approved lists.

options.text: To generate API tokens for developers.
options.explanation: API tokens are retrieved via the '•••' menu on a specific connection, not via the Members tab.

options.text: To install partner integrations like Zapier and Typeform.
options.explanation: Partner integrations are installed via OAuth or token linking, not specifically through the Members tab.

options.text: To manage Security & Compliance settings for the entire workspace.
options.explanation: Security & Compliance integrations are managed on the Workspaces tab, not the Members tab.
